summaries.Nadia Khasyshyn: Nadia Khasyshyn had a broad scope spanning CV/ML pipelines, embedded systems, and tooling. On the detection side she evaluated the current classifier against recent field footage, benchmarked Hailo YOLO models on a large dataset, ran evaluation on the Hailo simulator, investigated Locate Anything for auto-annotation, and tuned the zoom factor for the new model. In embedded/OS work she delivered an informative OSD for Вартовий with CPU optimization, integrated a second camera into the optical-flow process, and surfaced dual-camera output to Godot. On tooling and system reliability she implemented programmatic process-restart retries, a log-copy mechanism from RPI, an attack-invalidation time-window constraint, build-configurable mission support, flash expiry timestamps, and audited the dataset_tool for dataset-generation errors.
summaries.Mr Lab: Mr Lab concentrated on hardware integration, field readiness, and secure deployment. He reflashed drones to a secure-boot system image, researched compass degradation detection or mitigation ahead of flight, and prepared a drone for handover to 5ОШБр. On the hardware integration side he integrated an analog video encryptor and a thermal camera on Азимут for military operators. The work is primarily lab-and-field engineering with a focus on physical device configuration and mission-critical handoff.
summaries.Bohdan Forostianyi: Bohdan Forostianyi focused on communication reliability and system configuration for the drone platform. He pre-configured Ardu parameters for the custom optical-flow setup, built a guaranteed-delivery mechanism for payloads larger than 96 bytes over radio, and created a service that allows remote modification of configuration files via radio link. He also hardened the RPI environment by disabling WiFi and Bluetooth to reduce interference and attack surface.
summaries.Danylo Tomashy: Danylo Tomashy worked on flight controller communication and telemetry infrastructure. His focus was on the data pipeline between onboard components: one task covered sending metric-reset commands from RAM to InfluxDB via a stack mechanism, and another investigated a DATA96 command routing issue where RPI could read data from the FC but commands were not being forwarded to the flight controller. Both items point to low-level inter-process and radio-link protocol work.
